arguments.query: Devin AI software engineer API developer documentation
Devin AI API authentication OAuth2 API key developer access
Devin MCP server model context protocol official
Devin API access plan requirement free vs teams pricing

researched_category.value: AI Software Engineering Agent / Developer Tools
researched_category.notes: Devin is an autonomous AI software engineer that can write, run and test code, handle code migrations, bug fixes, PR reviews, and other software engineering tasks.

one_liner.value: Devin is an autonomous AI software engineer that can write, run and test code, handle bug fixes, code migrations, PR reviews, and other software engineering tasks autonomously.
one_liner.notes: From the official docs: 'Devin is the AI software engineer, built to help ambitious engineering teams crush their backlogs.'

methods.notes: Primary auth is via Service User API keys (cog_ prefix) passed as Bearer token in Authorization header. Personal Access Tokens (PATs) in closed beta. No OAuth2 support documented.

access.notes: Free plan exists but Devin API access requires at least a Teams plan ($80/month). The Teams quickstart guide explicitly targets teams/standard org users. Individual Free/Pro/Max plans do not appear to include REST API access. Enterprise plan available for larger orgs.

summary.value: Well-documented REST API (v3) at api.devin.ai/v3/ with Organization API (sessions, knowledge, playbooks, secrets, schedules, integrations) and Enterprise API (cross-org management, analytics, audit logs, billing, user management). Also has an official MCP server at mcp.devin.ai. Legacy v1/v2 APIs also documented but deprecated.
summary.notes: API docs cover session management (CRUD, messaging, events), knowledge base, playbooks, schedules, secrets, integrations, enterprise org/user/billing management, and webhook-like events.

agent_interface.notes: Official Devin MCP server exists at mcp.devin.ai providing session management, playbooks, knowledge, schedules, and integrations. DeepWiki MCP (no-auth, public repos) also available. Composio has a Devin MCP toolkit. CLI tool available.

rationale.value: Devin has a well-documented public REST API (v3), an official MCP server, and SDK/CLI tooling. The API is broad and covers all major Devin capabilities (sessions, knowledge, playbooks, schedules, integrations). The primary blocker is that API access requires a paid Teams plan ($80/month) - it is not available on the Free plan. However, for a developer willing to pay for a Teams subscription, building an agent toolkit for Devin is fully feasible today. An official MCP server already exists, and Composio has a Devin MCP toolkit.
rationale.notes: The only material blocker is the paid-plan gate for API access; technically the integration surface is complete and well-documented.

s1.quote: Devin is the AI software engineer, built to help ambitious engineering teams crush their backlogs. Devin is an autonomous AI software engineer that can write, run and test code.
s1.title: Introducing Devin - Devin Docs

s3.quote: The Devin API uses a principal + token model... Service User API Key... Personal Access Token (PAT)... All API credentials use the cog_ prefix format. Include your token in the Authorization header of every request: Authorization: Bearer cog_your_token_here
s3.title: Authentication - Devin Docs

s4.quote: Personal Access Tokens are currently in closed beta and are feature-flagged. Contact support to request access.
s4.title: Personal Access Tokens [Closed Beta] - Devin Docs

s5.quote: Free $0... Pro $20/month... Max $200/month... Teams $80/month... Enterprise Let's talk
s5.title: Plans and Pricing | Devin

s6.quote: Free: Individuals trying Devin... Pro: Individual users... Max: Power users... Teams: Teams working with Devin together
s6.title: Self-serve plans - Devin Docs

s7.quote: Get started with the Devin API as a teams or standard organization user
s7.title: Quick start: Teams - Devin Docs

s8.quote: The Devin API enables you to integrate Devin into your applications, automate workflows, and build powerful tools. Use service users with role-based access control for secure, auditable API access.
s8.title: API Overview - Devin Docs

s9.quote: API Overview... Authentication... Common Flows... Pagination... Quick start: Enterprise... Quick start: Teams... Personal Access Tokens... v1 API... Sessions, Knowledge, Playbooks, Secrets, etc.
s9.title: Devin Docs Index

s10.quote: The Devin MCP server provides programmatic access to Devin's platform capabilities for both private and public repositories. Base Server URL: https://mcp.devin.ai/
s10.title: Devin MCP - Devin Docs

s11.quote: Available Tools: Repository Documentation (read_wiki_structure, read_wiki_contents, ask_question, list_available_repos), Session Management (create, search, interact, events, gather), Playbook Management, Knowledge Management, Schedule Management, Integration Management
s11.title: Devin MCP - Devin Docs

s12.quote: Devin MCP MCP for AI Agents. Securely connect your AI agents and chatbots (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc) with Devin MCP MCP or direct API
s12.title: Devin MCP MCP Integration for AI Agents

limitations: The exact feature/plan matrix for API access (whether Pro or Max plans also include the REST API) could not be definitively determined from pricing page content alone; the Teams quickstart is the entry-level API guide. Personal Access Tokens are in closed beta.
